meta.title: Veo 3.1 API | APIDot
meta.description: Access Google DeepMind Veo 3.1 on APIDot for 8-second video generation with native audio, reference images, start/end frame control, and Fast, Lite, or Quality pricing tiers.

hero.title: Veo 3.1 Playground
hero.description: Test Veo 3.1 Fast, Lite, and Quality on APIDot with native audio, reference-guided video generation, and the same async API flow you can ship directly into production.

overview.title: What is {modelName}?
paragraphs: {modelName} is Google DeepMind's video generation family built for cinematic short-form clips with synchronized native audio, stronger prompt adherence, and more deliberate control over motion, framing, and scene continuity.

On APIDot, Veo 3.1 is available through Fast, Lite, and Quality variants for 8-second jobs with 16:9 or 9:16 output, reference-image workflows, and async delivery. The currently selected variant is {activeModelId}, billed at {priceLabel}.

items.title: Native audio with scene-aware sync
items.description: Generate dialogue, ambient sound, and effects together with the video instead of stitching audio later. {modelName} is designed for short clips that already feel finished when they come out of the model.

items.title: Text, ingredients, and frames-to-video workflows
items.description: Start from a pure text prompt, upload reference images for character and style consistency, or use first and last frames to guide transitions. This mirrors the multi-input workflows that made Veo 3.1 stand out on PoYo.

items.title: Director-style camera and motion control
items.description: Use film language such as dolly, tracking, overhead, portrait framing, lighting cues, and shot composition to push the model toward more intentional results instead of generic motion.

items.title: Fast, Lite, and Quality tiers
items.description: Choose Fast for quick iteration, Lite for the lowest per-video cost, or Quality when fidelity matters most. The broader Veo 3.1 family also carries production-oriented safeguards such as SynthID watermarking in Google's ecosystem.

useCases.title: Where {modelName} fits best
useCases.description: Teams use Veo 3.1 when they need native audio, faster iteration, and stronger control than prompt-only video tools usually provide. Common production patterns include:
items: Localized ad and campaign video generation
E-commerce product demos from reference images
Game trailers, cutscenes, and virtual world concept clips

steps.title: Create your API key
steps.description: Generate an APIDot API key once and use it as the Bearer token for all Veo 3.1 Fast, Lite, and Quality requests.

steps.title: Add credits for the tier you want
steps.description: Top up credits based on the Veo 3.1 variant and resolution you plan to run. Fast is optimized for iteration, Lite for cost efficiency, and Quality for premium output.

steps.title: Submit async jobs and retrieve results
steps.description: POST to {endpoint} with model="{activeModelId}", get a task ID back, then poll status or use callbacks to collect the finished video when the job completes.

items.question: What is Veo 3.1 on APIDot?
items.answer: It is APIDot's unified access point for Google DeepMind Veo 3.1 Fast, Lite, and Quality. You can submit 8-second generation jobs with text prompts, reference images, or frame-guided inputs through one async API flow.

items.question: Which inputs and generation modes are supported?
items.answer: This page supports prompt-only generation plus image-guided workflows. In practice that means text-to-video, reference-image generation, and first-frame or first-and-last-frame guided video generation for more controlled scene transitions.

items.question: What output settings are available on this page?
items.answer: The current APIDot Veo 3.1 page is built around 8-second videos, 16:9 or 9:16 aspect ratios, and 720p, 1080p, or 4K output depending on the variant you select.

items.question: Does Veo 3.1 generate native audio, and do failed jobs get charged?
items.answer: Yes. Native audio is one of the headline strengths of the Veo 3.1 family. On APIDot, credits are intended for successful generations rather than failed jobs.

items.question: Does the API use an async job pattern?
items.answer: Yes. You submit a job, receive a task ID, then poll the status endpoint or handle a callback when generation finishes. That keeps the integration practical for queue-based video workloads.

items.question: How does Veo 3.1 compare with Veo 3 or Sora 2?
items.answer: Veo 3.1 is generally chosen for native audio, stronger reference control, and more structured cinematic prompting. Sora 2 is often favored for single-shot realism, while Veo 3.1 is especially compelling when audio, ingredients, and frame guidance matter.
